videos.title: Hungary elections: Is this the end of Orbán? | ARTE Europe Weekly
videos.description:
Hungary’s Prime Minister Viktor Orbán could be unseated after 16 years in power. Few EU leaders would miss him – for years, he’s been the black sheep of Europe, blocking key decisions and support for Ukraine. Domestically, he’s led a brutal crackdown on the judiciary, the media and the rights of minorities. With elections in other EU countries on the horizon, this election is being seen as a litmus test for Europe. Will right-wing populism triumph – or is this the end of the “illiberal democracy” in the heart of Europe?

Hungary elections: Is this the end of Orbán? | ARTE Europe Weekly

videos.title: Hungary: A Far-Right Testing Ground | ARTE.tv Documentary
videos.description:

His illiberal politics of hollowing out democratic institutions, attacking independent media and scapegoating minorities are widely celebrated by far-right commentators across the world. But it is in Europe where his influence is most present, as Orban is now exporting his ideology to countries across the EU.

videos.title: Is water being sacrificed for profit? | ARTE Europe Weekly
videos.description:
For 25 years, the European Union’s water protection has long rested on a single piece of legislation: the Water Framework Directive. The European Commission is now advancing a so-called Water Resilience Strategy and weighing changes to the 2000 directive that would ease the way for industry. Environmental groups, meanwhile, are raising alarms, insisting that water must remain a resource unlike any other.
